summary: Iteration 5 verified end-to-end. Backend: 37/37 pytest pass — new iter5 test file covers GET /api/staff (experience_cities present, response_count absent, months is int), GET /api/stats (exactly 3 keys: active_personnel/teams_in_cities/years_in_service, no total_responses, no applications_received; teams_in_cities equals distinct city count derived from staff.experience_cities), POST /api/admin/staff with experience_cities round-trips and persists, PATCH /api/admin/staff/{id} updates experience_cities and persists, type validation returns 422 on both POST and PATCH when months='abc', and 'response_count' sent by a client is silently dropped (extra='ignore'). Updated iter2 test_pillbox_api.py to drop legacy response_count references and adjust stats key set. Frontend (Playwright on preview URL): /home stats grid has 3 tiles (Active Personnel=1, Teams In Cities=2, Years In Service=8), no 'Total Responses' or standalone 'Applications' tile. /roster opens Ms Trisha's detail modal — 'Responses' row is GONE, City Experience section renders with [data-testid=staff-detail-cities] showing Los Santos · SENIOR · 18 mo and Paleto Bay · LEAD · 6 mo. /admin StaffEditor: no 'Response Count' input; editor-cities block + city-add-btn create a row with city-name-{i} / city-grade-{i} / city-months-{i} / city-remove-{i}; saving TEST_UI Iter5 with city Strawberry/Expert/15 persists — re-opening the editor reads back the row exactly. Cleanup deletes the test staff. IDCard component scanned: no response_count references in any frontend source file.

issues: Dialog console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for DialogContent' — accessibility nit carried over from prior iterations, not introduced by iter5.

action_items: Optional: add a DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to StaffDetailModal to silence the Radix accessibility warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py CityExperience model uses int for months with default 0 — Pydantic accepts numeric strings ('12') and coerces them, but rejects non-numeric ('abc') with 422. Matches the spec.

GET /api/stats uses db.staff.distinct('experience_cities.city') — returns distinct city values across the array elements. Empty strings would technically count as a distinct value; current data only has non-empty cities so this is fine, but a server-side aggregation filter ({$ne: ''}) would be safer if blank cities ever leak.
Startup migration $unset response_count and $set experience_cities=[] when missing — idempotent and safe. Note FastAPI @app.on_event('startup') is deprecated; lifespan is recommended (carried over).

AdminPage.normalize() trims city/grade and Number()-coerces months, filtering out rows without a city — good defensive layer, but the empty-city rows are silently dropped client-side; consider surfacing a hint to the admin.
StaffMember has extra='ignore' so a client posting response_count is dropped silently. Confirmed via test_no_response_count_in_admin_create_response.
Home.jsx stats grid is grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 — confirmed 3 tiles render.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_pillbox_api.py (updated: removed response_count from REQUIRED_STAFF_FIELDS + NEW_STAFF_PAYLOAD, updated /api/stats expected keys, relaxed seed-count assertion since DB has 1 staff per agent context)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (home 3-stat grid, modal city section, admin editor city CRUD round-trip)

seed_data_creation: All test data is created and cleaned up within each test (TEST_Iter5 Cities/Patch/BadMonths/BadCreate/ExtraField + UI-created TEST_UI Iter5 deleted via UI). Final DB state matches pre-test state (1 staff: Ms Trisha with 2 experience_cities).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter5 test file is /app/backend/tests/test_pillbox_iter5.py. test_pillbox_api.py was updated to remove obsolete response_count references and to relax the '>=8 seeded' assertion to '>=1' since the seeder only runs when staff collection is empty and the admin has already replaced the seed with a single live staff member (Ms Trisha, EMS-01, P-01) with experience_cities=[Los Santos/Senior/18, Paleto Bay/Lead/6]. /api/stats currently returns {active_personnel:1, teams_in_cities:2, years_in_service:8}. Browser headless_shell failed once mid-suite with SIGSEGV when re-launching for /idcard route — flake unrelated to app code; iframe scan of source confirmed zero response_count references anywhere in frontend src.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All iteration-5 acceptance criteria satisfied: response_count fully removed from backend model/storage/UI/ID card; experience_cities added with proper validation; /api/stats returns exactly 3 keys with teams_in_cities computed from distinct cities; admin editor has city sub-editor with all required testids; staff modal shows City Experience section with data-testid=staff-detail-cities.
